steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form an independent clause about Auerbach disputing claims. The words after the blank begin with an introductory participial phrase and lead into another independent clause about his argument.

steps.title: Use strong punctuation
steps.content: Choice A joins the parts without needed punctuation. Choice B creates a comma splice. Choice C changes the meaning by making "citing" part of a prepositional phrase after "might." The remaining choice correctly separates the independent clauses with a semicolon. The answer is D.
